assumptions: The canonical decision model (direction + strength) is the source of truth for all new domain analysis aggregation.
win-rate = prioritized / (prioritized + deprioritized) is a valid canonical metric derivable from canonical decisions.
Bradley-Terry (FULL_BT) and log-odds scores derived from canonical win/loss counts are valid ranking metrics.
Cosine similarity over canonical-decision-derived score vectors is valid for the similarity table.
meanDecisionScore is legacy (average of 1-5 decisionCode values) and has no canonical equivalent — it is removed.
orientationFlipped is stored on the Scenario record and must be batch-loaded to resolve canonical decisions correctly.
unknown direction decisions (where the canonical model cannot determine a decision) are excluded from aggregation counts.
Export, worker, MCP, and assumption surfaces are out of scope for this slice.
Transcript and run surfaces (TranscriptList, RunDetail, etc.) are out of scope for this slice.
discovery.summary: Replace the legacy decisionCode-based aggregation in the domain analysis backend with canonical decision model resolution. The score shape (log-odds, BT, win-rate) stays but is now derived from canonical decisions rather than raw 1-5 decisionCode parsing. Remove meanDecisionScore as it has no canonical equivalent.
non_goals: Do not change the score field type or remove log-odds/BT score options from the UI.
Do not change export, worker, MCP, assumptions, or order-invariance surfaces.
Do not change transcript/run list display surfaces.
Do not remove the scoreMethod arg from GraphQL.
Do not redesign the similarity table algorithm.
acceptance_criteria: domainAnalysis aggregation reads decisionMetadata + definitionSnapshot + orientationFlipped to resolve canonical decisions.
decisionCode: { in: ['1','2','3','4','5'] } filter is removed from all three domain analysis queries.
meanDecisionScore field is removed from DomainAnalysisConditionDetail type, GraphQL schema, queries, and frontend.
prioritized/deprioritized/neutral counts are derived from canonical direction, not decisionCode integer parsing.
unknown-direction decisions are excluded from counts (not treated as neutral).
